sourceMovie.title: Summer Palace
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Summer Palace (2006) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Life in a Year
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Summer Palace clicked for you, Life in a Year (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Summer Palace?
faq.answer: Try Life in a Year, Time Is Up, Chéri—each shares DNA with Summer Palace while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Summer Palace?
faq.answer: Summer Palace (2006) is best described as Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Summer Palace worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
